old_claim.note: Printed standardized textbooks were treated as an enabling capability for public science lectures without direct edge-level source support.

new_claim_absent: No direct printed-textbook dependency remains because the public lecture node is scoped to civic spoken demonstrations and endowed teaching, not textbook standardization.

source_shape.source_locator: Gresham College page: City of London presents the institution through public lectures and its civic foundation, without identifying printed standardized textbooks as required infrastructure.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports public lectures as Gresham College's civic educational function.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Textbooks are related educational media; a lecture institution should not inherit a direct textbook-standardization prerequisite without source support.

ontology_before: Public Science Lectures depended directly on Printed Standardized Textbooks, implying standardized printed course media were required for the lecture program.
ontology_after: Public Science Lectures keeps its source-backed civic lecture scope and no longer directly depends on textbook standardization.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: printed_standardized_textbooks is no longer a direct prerequisite for public_science_lectures.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to printed science curricula rather than public lectures.
A stronger source showed standardized textbooks directly enabled the 1597 Gresham lecture program.
A dependent required public_science_lectures to represent textbook-mediated instruction rather than civic spoken demonstrations.
short_reason: Printed textbooks are educational media, not the direct lecture prerequisite.
